title: Question: tracing   with SystemC and SystemPerl
body:
---


Author Name: **Mark Jackson Pulver**
Original Redmine Message: 1208 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Still just learing to use these tools.

Trying to learn how to trace signals.  
I had trouble linking code generated with SystemPerl.
Looking deep in the difference between test_sc & test_sp (and sc_main.ccp),
I discovered instead of including verilated_vcd_sc.cpp verilated_vcd_c.cpp in the compile it reqiure Sp.cpp.

There seems to be no mention of Sp.cpp in the verilator man pages.

Here's a fragment of my final makefile:
```
VFLAGS=-DVERILATOR=1
ifdef TRACE
ifdef SYSPERL
VFILES += Sp.cpp
else
VFILES +=verilated_vcd_sc.cpp verilated_vcd_c.cpp
endif
VFLAGS +=--trace
endif
VK_GLOBAL_OBJS += ${VFILES:%.cpp=%.o}
VK_GLOBAL_OBJS += verilated.o

ifdef SYSPERL
obj_dir/%.sp: %.vdpends
	verilator ${VFLAGS} -Wall --sp --exe  $*.v ${VFILES}

obj_dir/%.cpp: obj_dir/%.sp
	cd obj_dir;  ${SYSTEMPERL}/sp_preproc --preproc *.sp
else
obj_dir/%.cpp: %.vdpends
	verilator ${VFLAGS} -Wall --sc --exe  $*.v ${VFILES}
endif

obj_dir/V%__ALL.a:  obj_dir/%.cpp
	${MAKE} -C obj_dir -j -f V$*.mk V$*__ALL.a

%_tb.o: %_tb.cpp obj_dir/V%__ALL.a
	 ${MAKE} -C obj_dir -j -f V$*.mk ../$*_tb.o ${VK_GLOBAL_OBJS}

%_tb: %_tb.o
	g++ -L${SYSTEMC_LIBDIR} $*_tb.o ${VK_GLOBAL_OBJS:%=obj_dir/%} obj_dir/V$*__ALL*.o -o $*_tb -lsystemc -lm -lstdc++ 	

```

It assumes   the testbech for module XXX  is in XXX_tb.cpp

so make TRACE=1 SYSPERL=1  XXX_tb  creates the exe  using SYSTEMPERL generation stream
and make TRACE=1 XXX_tb creates the exe using straight SystemC generation stream.

I beleive the man page could use a bit about why SystemPerl verus SystemC.  Looks like coverage only works under SystemPerl.

Mark
code_blocks:

ifdef TRACE
ifdef SYSPERL
VFILES += Sp.cpp
else

VK_GLOBAL_OBJS += ${VFILES:%.cpp=%.o}

ifdef SYSPERL
obj_dir/%.sp: %.vdpends
	verilator ${VFLAGS} -Wall --sp --exe  $*.v ${VFILES}

obj_dir/%.cpp: obj_dir/%.sp
	cd obj_dir;  ${SYSTEMPERL}/sp_preproc --preproc *.sp
else
obj_dir/%.cpp: %.vdpends
	verilator ${VFLAGS} -Wall --sc --exe  $*.v ${VFILES}
endif

obj_dir/V%__ALL.a:  obj_dir/%.cpp
	${MAKE} -C obj_dir -j -f V$*.mk V$*__ALL.a

%_tb.o: %_tb.cpp obj_dir/V%__ALL.a
	 ${MAKE} -C obj_dir -j -f V$*.mk ../$*_tb.o ${VK_GLOBAL_OBJS}

%_tb: %_tb.o
	g++ -L${SYSTEMC_LIBDIR} $*_tb.o ${VK_GLOBAL_OBJS:%=obj_dir/%} obj_dir/V$*__ALL*.o -o $*_tb -lsystemc -lm -lstdc++
